objections.issue: T24 (ReadyForJointView) is missing T25 in its depends_on. T24's own ACs state: "'Enter Joint Session →' is a large primary button; clicking it calls a mutation to transition the case to JOINT_ACTIVE and navigates to the joint chat." That mutation is jointChat/enterJointChat, which is implemented in T25 (joint chat Convex mutations: sendUserMessage, enterJointChat, proposeClosure). T24's current depends_on is ["T11", "T43"] — T25 is absent. Without T25 completed, T24 has no mutation to invoke when the user clicks 'Enter Joint Session'.
objections.fix: Add T25 to T24's depends_on. The resulting depends_on should be ["T11", "T43", "T25"]. No other changes are required — the round-3 objection (T26 missing T43) has been correctly resolved.

summary: Round 4 correctly carries the single fix from round 3: T26's depends_on now includes T43, resolving the mySynthesis query dependency. The plan is structurally sound in coverage (all 15 P0/P1 user stories covered), NFR library (6 NFRs matching all PRD sections), NFR application, Phase 0 completeness (5 human-gate tickets each with verified setup ACs), sizing (all 48 tickets carry exactly one surface), Playwright ownership, testing realism (convex-test for Convex functions, Vitest for utilities, Playwright E2E for feature flows), design fidelity, privacy/security fidelity, and decision surfacing. One dependency edge is still missing: T24 (ReadyForJointView) calls the enterJointChat mutation from T25, but T25 is not listed in T24's depends_on. This is structurally identical to the T26/T43 gap found in round 3 — a frontend page that invokes a mutation without declaring the mutation's ticket as a dependency. Fix is a single-line change: add T25 to T24's depends_on.
